misconceptionSummary: Students treat formulae as ordinary expressions and miss that each symbol stands for a quantity in a relationship.

visuals.ruleText: A formula is a rule linking quantities
visuals.subText: Each letter stands for a specific quantity

steps.narration: A formula is more than an expression. It tells us how different quantities are related.

lines.text: l means length, w means width

steps.narration: In a formula, the letters have named roles. They are not interchangeable because each one represents a different quantity.

lines.text: If l = 8 and w = 2, then A = 8 × 2

steps.narration: Substitution into a formula means replacing each symbol with the matching quantity it stands for.

lines.text: This is not just a number, it is the area

steps.narration: The answer also has meaning. It tells you the value of the quantity named on the left-hand side of the formula.

visuals.expression: Use the meaning of each symbol as well as the algebra
visuals.label: Formulae connect quantities

steps.narration: That is why formula substitution matters. It connects the algebra to the quantities in the context.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: swapping the meanings of symbols or ignoring what the formula represents. In formulae, letters are labels for specific quantities.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Do not treat the letters as generic placeholders only. In a formula, each one names a quantity in a real relationship.
